title: Control Module
possible_causes: Control module internal status, reset history, power supply stability, shutdown sequence, ground, network wake/sleep state, software/configuration, or related module influence may be involved depending on the vehicle.
Internal failure criteria, shutdown timing, reset counters, connector pins, voltage thresholds, and recovery procedures are vehicle-specific and must be confirmed in official service information.
check_order: Save all-module DTCs, freeze-frame data, module reset/shutdown history if available, module part numbers, calibration IDs, configuration data, battery voltage, ignition state, wake/sleep state, and related power, ground, or communication DTCs.

Confirm vehicle-specific module power-down logic, retained accessory power behavior, network sleep/wake prerequisites, software compatibility, coding/configuration, and diagnostic prerequisites in the manufacturer service manual or OEM service system.
Inspect battery condition, charging voltage, module power feeds, grounds, relays, fuses, connectors, harness condition, water intrusion, and network-related DTCs before replacing modules.

Separate module internal fault, improper power-down, low or unstable supply voltage, ground fault, relay/input circuit fault, wake/sleep logic issue, configuration mismatch, and network influence using the manufacturer procedure.
measurement_points: Control module power/ground and shutdown data
common_mistakes: Replacing a module before checking battery support, power feeds, grounds, wake/sleep behavior, relays, and configuration data.
Assuming shutdown criteria, voltage thresholds, connector terminals, or reset-history meaning are common across vehicles.
Clearing or programming modules without saving the pre-repair scan, configuration data, and recovery information.
required_tools: Power distribution and network diagnostic tools
safety_notes: Use strong caution around high-voltage, SRS/restraints, brake, steering, fuel, ignition, charging, and programming/coding related systems; unstable power or incorrect configuration can disable safety-critical modules, so follow OEM disable, support, initialization, and recovery procedures.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance
